title: Hardware profile (fleet schema; Contract 3/7 input)
description: Describes one hardware configuration for capacity modeling (fleetlab) and benchmark cross-reference. PROVENANCE IS MANDATORY AND STRUCTURAL: every quantitative fact is a provenanced value object ({value, provenance}) — a bare number is not schema-valid, so fabricated defaults are not expressible. Identity fields (ids, names, model strings) are identities, not measurements, and carry no provenance. Hardware specs and prices are volatile ecosystem facts (as of 2026-07 — re-verify at use time).

version.description: Profile version; bump when any value changes (profiles are pinned by consumers).

instance_type.description: Cloud instance type identity (e.g. g5.xlarge) or bare-metal SKU.

interconnect.description: Multi-GPU interconnect identity (e.g. PCIe Gen4, NVLink); relevant only when count_per_node > 1.

provenance.description: MANDATORY provenance: how this value was established and when. basis=measured: from an identified measurement (source = benchmark result_id or measurement artifact). basis=source-reported: from an identified external source (vendor spec, pricing page, paper). basis=assumed: an explicit assumption — never a silent default; state the reasoning in notes.

source.description: REQUIRED for measured and source-reported values: what produced or reported the value.
